message:
feat: add `dcg setup` command with shell startup hook-removal detection

Introduces a new `setup` subcommand that combines hook installation with an
optional shell RC file check. The shell check is a lightweight snippet injected
into ~/.bashrc and/or ~/.zshrc that warns on every new terminal session if the
dcg PreToolUse hook has been silently removed from Claude Code's settings.json.

This addresses a real operational risk: Claude Code can rewrite settings.json
during normal operation, silently dropping the dcg safety hook without any
user-visible indication. The shell check runs in milliseconds (jq one-liner),
is completely silent when the hook is present, and only prints a yellow warning
when it detects the hook is missing.

Implementation details:
- DCG_SHELL_CHECK_SNIPPET: the shell snippet with jq-based detection
- rc_has_dcg_check(): idempotent presence check via marker comment
- inject_shell_check(): append-only injection with create-if-missing
- run_setup(): orchestrates hook install + interactive/flag-driven RC injection
- Supports --shell-check (auto-yes), --no-shell-check (skip), and interactive
  prompt via inquire::Confirm for TTY sessions
- CLI flags: `dcg setup [--force] [--shell-check] [--no-shell-check]`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
